characters.description: Karen's police commissioner, responsible for public order as the city absorbs displaced Chicago residents. The commissioner accepts Tatiana's offer of outside help and invites the visiting group to a government employees' bowling night.
characters.name: Karen's police commissioner

characters.description: Karen's founder and the master served by the animal-featured visitors whom Tatiana recruits to address unrest among the city's new arrivals.
characters.name: Randidly Ghosthound

characters.description: Randidly's sentient armor. It covers the damaged replacement arm and the painful runes extending from his arm to his shoulder.
characters.name: Sulphur

characters.description: Karen's practical civic leader and Randidly's chief administrator. She recruits an intimidating group of his followers to address unrest among the city's new arrivals.
characters.name: Tatiana

characters.description: Randidly's soulbound companion and an Aether specialist. She is working with Sydney in the Toad world when Randidly receives his first briefing after returning to Earth.
characters.name: Nevaeh

characters.description: A Valkyrie and experienced Earth defender. Together with Hank, she is tracking the controller of a murderous blood avatar that previously attacked Zone One's president.
characters.name: Alana

characters.description: An Earth defender pursuing the controller of a murderous blood avatar alongside Alana after the pair prevented an attack on Zone One's president.
characters.name: Hank Howard

characters.description: An associate of Nevaeh who is with her in the Toad world when they report that the immediate situation there is controlled.
characters.name: Sydney

characters.description: A combat-focused subordinate of Randidly and custodian of Karen's elite order. She brings the captured James Kelly to Karen and challenges Randidly to a spear spar.
characters.name: Helen

characters.description: A former professional basketball player who leads the surviving Chicago Chosen. His karmic network redirects his pain and injuries into his followers.
characters.name: James Kelly

characters.description: Kirstie's twin brother. He joins her and two veteran protectors on a trip to encourage Mariah Jane during her football halftime performance, and believes his ability might help the young archer adopt a singing class.
characters.name: Nathan

characters.description: Nathan's twin sister. She travels with him, Tykes, and Dinesh to encourage Mariah Jane during the singer's scheduled football halftime performance.
characters.name: Kirstie

characters.description: A sixteen-year-old halftime performer with an archer class and a connection to Nathan, who believes his ability might help her pursue a singing-related class.
characters.name: Mariah Jane

characters.description: One of Nathan's experienced protectors. He accompanies Nathan and Kirstie to support Mariah Jane at her scheduled football halftime performance.
characters.name: Dinesh

characters.description: One of Nathan's experienced protectors. He travels with Nathan, Kirstie, and Dinesh to support Mariah Jane at her football halftime performance.
characters.name: Tykes

characters.description: Zone One's president and chairperson of the World Council. She receives a warning of a credible attack on the inaugural football game after Karen's elite order declines to provide guards.
characters.name: Theodora Grayman

characters.description: A Zone One guard assigned to protect and monitor quarterback Jake Tuck during the inaugural football game.
characters.name: Ezekiel

characters.description: The East Providence Stallions quarterback. He aims to make himself and his team the leading public face of the revived football league.
characters.name: Jake Tuck

characters.description: A grievously injured survivor receiving treatment for a chaotic fate. His suffering drives Sydney to seek vengeance against those she considers responsible.
characters.name: Drake

characters.description: An energy surgeon treating Drake with guidance informed by Nathan's advice. She can restrain his chaotic fate but cannot safely remake it.
characters.name: Regina Northwind

characters.description: A widowed Zone One border officer and Timothy's father. He accepts an unfair school ruling rather than risk his son's expulsion, even as the decision further erodes his faith in Zone One's institutions.
characters.name: Derek Moss

characters.description: Derek's son, suspended after striking a well-connected bully. His father accepts an unfair ruling rather than risk Tim's expulsion and remains determined to keep him safe.
characters.name: Timothy Moss

characters.description: Annie's partner and Delilah's father. He joins the ogre-settlement delegation preparing to confront the ruler of the large army beyond the gorge.
characters.name: Dozer

characters.description: Annie and Dozer's daughter, about five years old. She is already trained and armed when Randidly meets her at the ogre settlement.
characters.name: Delilah

characters.description: Dozer's partner and Delilah's mother. She joins the Ogre Gorge group meeting Randidly about the immense army waiting beyond the gorge.
characters.name: Annie

characters.description: A Red Revival representative involved in the concealed operation beneath Zone One's inaugural football game.
characters.name: Declan Hyde

characters.description: Leader of the Earth-bound Nemesai involved in the stadium conspiracy. Her image remains unrestricted, unlike those of her companions.
characters.name: Thea

characters.description: A Red Revival representative coordinating with the Nemesai beneath the stadium. His group plans to slaughter the football crowd and collect their blood for a ritual.
characters.name: Stroud

characters.description: Randidly's living plant spear, carried when he travels to confront the ruler of the Nordon force.
characters.name: Acri

characters.description: Nordon's unnamed three-headed ruler. He sets aside his ceremonial identity and challenges Randidly as the Ogrelord when the floating island arrives above his army.
characters.name: The Ogrelord

characters.description: Randidly's father. He unknowingly holds a required piece of Randidly's fate set, which Randidly can obtain only if his father gives it freely.
characters.name: Randidly's father

characters.description: The power behind Kirstie's priestess abilities, expressed through a hostile treant-like image when she attacks Stroud.
characters.name: Arbor

characters.description: The dragon broodmother Alana rides into the sealed stadium during the defense against the blood ritual and the Nemesai attack.
characters.name: Wyvanya

characters.description: A winged-serpent Nemesai that manifests during the stadium assault and activates the blood-gathering stone-wheel array.
characters.name: The winged-serpent Nemesai

characters.description: The three-headed ogre lord whom Randidly recently defeated. He is identified during the report on the stadium disaster and the enormous ogre migration now approaching Karen.
characters.name: Wolfram

characters.description: Randidly's sea soul seed, an ocean-spanning thorned seaweed organism. Thorn grieves the dead dagger fighter, whom it regarded as its closest relationship outside Randidly.
characters.name: Thorn

characters.description: A dormant remnant within Randidly's Ignition Essence, associated with phoenix power but not yet forming a coherent center for the image.
characters.name: Elim

characters.description: A recruiter for Karen's elite order. Naffer interviews Derek after he and Tim arrive in the city seeking a safer home and a path toward greater strength.
characters.name: Naffer Sweet

characters.description: An ancient enemy using Lyra's appearance. She has spread an Aether territory across the Endless Sea and appears to be preparing a trap as part of an effort to escape the System.
characters.name: The woman wearing Lyra's face

characters.description: The leader whose destructive image maintains discipline among the hunted Nemesai. She regards Randidly's birthday invitation as genuine and sees it as a possible opportunity.
characters.name: Thea Glasshammer

characters.description: A Donnyton representative coordinating its delegation to Randidly's birthday gathering with Donnie and helping select the settlement's gift.
characters.name: Mrs. Hamilton

characters.description: Randidly's Nexus contact, who reports that the Council presented Earth's corrupted-invader scenario as a reward and warns that it carries concealed danger.
characters.name: Octavius Shrike

characters.description: An undead Hero under Nevaeh's care. He attends Randidly's birthday gathering while resenting her control and looking for a chance to escape it.
characters.name: Roy

characters.description: A visitor from the Alpha Cosmos whom Randidly invites to his birthday gathering because her past actions illustrate the System's consequences.
characters.name: Alta Bounty

characters.description: Randidly's former best friend, now pursuing a secretive blood-powered project. He has stolen magical-plastic research, an Impending Rock, and lifeblood essence to construct an egg-like creation.
characters.name: Ace

characters.description: A deceased former enemy appearing in a memory reached through the Dreamcatcher. In that remembered encounter, she tried to appropriate Randidly's images and Aether crossroads for her own refuge.
characters.name: Yestix

characters.description: A Nexus investigator expected to interfere with Earth's corrupted-invader trial. Randidly is warned that the official is stronger than his former military superior and may use the scenario to force Earth into failure.
characters.name: The Zurt Brigade special investigator

characters.description: Ace's ally, who recognizes that his fixation on opposing Randidly is becoming more severe. She remains beside him and admits that they took Yestix's missing memories.
characters.name: Rose Calloway

characters.description: A Karen diplomat and ritual user operating ahead of the moving city. Randidly orders Karen toward her position as a vast bubble-city caravan approaches.
characters.name: Vi

characters.description: Naffer Sweet's partner and Ed Dug's former assistant. She ends her job with Ed and relocates to Karen to join Naffer.
characters.name: Maureen

characters.description: A person known to Randidly whose appearance is being used by the ancient creature spreading Aether through the northern Endless Sea.
characters.name: Lyra

characters.description: The authoritarian leader of a bubble-city caravan numbering nearly half a million people. He approaches Karen after ruling a Moscow suburb harshly and is identified by the Blighted Paragon class.
characters.name: The blighted paragon

characters.description: The Nexus's supreme individual power. He interrupts an emergency council dispute, kills the specialist-force leader, and orders Earth protected because the planet troubles his incomplete memory.
characters.name: The Nexus pinnacle figure

characters.description: A powerful Nexus figure returning after a long absence. Distinct from the deceased woman tied to Yestix, she protects the seventh-cohort overseer from a powerful family's representative and resumes involvement in Randidly's affairs.
characters.name: The returned original woman
